search_queries_used: site:rcoa.ac.uk GPAS regional anesthesia follow-up discharge
site:asahq.org neuraxial opioid monitoring ventilation oxygenation consciousness
site:doh.gov.ae day surgery regional anesthesia follow-up
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic management or dose instructions for Neuraxial anesthesia follow-up
generic anesthesia landing pages without an exact section for Neuraxial anesthesia follow-up
rejection_reasons: Medicine dose and complication management require direct specialist assessment.
The candidate source category "automatic neurologic complication algorithms" did not provide exact documentation-only evidence for this workflow and was not mapped.
Autonomous management, medication, dose, procedure, escalation, or discharge instructions were outside the documentation-only scope.
Landing pages and search snippets were not accepted as exact evidence.
population_applicability: Patients receiving regional or neuraxial anesthesia; opioid-specific monitoring evidence applies only when neuraxial opioids were used.
setting_applicability: Direct for clinician-documented postprocedure follow-up; technique, medicine, and complication treatment remain outside scope.
UAE_applicability: International RCoA and ASA evidence requires UAE facility and specialty adaptation.
recency_verification: Current RCoA GPAS 2025 and official ASA neuraxial-opioid guideline published 2016 were reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: RCoA GPAS is current; no newer full ASA neuraxial-opioid guideline was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact RCoA section supports documented follow-up systems, mobilization, discharge information, and postoperative education after regional or neuraxial anesthesia.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documented follow-up systems, mobilization status, discharge information, and postoperative education without directing management.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact ASA preanesthesia documentation section supports risk, airway, cardiorespiratory, reviewed-investigation, consultation, plan, and consent fields for Neuraxial anesthesia follow-up; each remains clinician-entered and conditional on assessment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.

unresolved_source_gaps: Technique, level, medicine, dose, block characteristics, neurologic examination, urinary retention, headache, infection, hematoma, respiratory monitoring frequency, treatment, and escalation remain unsupported.
Legacy duration presets, broad generic interval-history wording, and any content not explicitly mapped to an exact section remain unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No source mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, anesthesia plan, investigation, procedure, medication, dose, referral, discharge decision, or urgency conclusion.
UAE facility policy, credentialing, pathway, and qualified anesthesiologist review remain required before use.

legacy_item_support_mappings.direct_relationship: The exact RCoA section supports documented follow-up systems, mobilization, discharge information, and postoperative education after regional or neuraxial anesthesia.
